content.full_text:
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

January 27, 1976

Raymond W. Schneider, County Counsel
Humboldt County
825 Fifth Street
Eureka, Calif. 95501

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-006

Dear Mr. Schneider:

     Thank you for your letter of January 12, 1976, requesting an opinion with respect to the application of the Conflict of Interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

     Opinions may be requested by "any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person's representative." 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18372(a). Since it is unclear from your letter whether you are writing as the representative of Mr. Lacy, no formal opinion can be issued by the Commission at this time. If you wish to resubmit your opinion request clarifying the relationship between you and Mr. Lacy, the Commission will again consider whether to issue a formal opinion.

     I also want to advise you that the Commission currently is considering a regulation which might resolve your problem. Accordingly, please find enclosed a copy of proposed regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18703. This regulation will be considered by the Commission during its meeting of February 3, 1976 through February 5, 1976. If you have any comments concerning this regulation we would appreciate receiving them prior to this meeting.

     If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. Also, if you have any further questions, please contact Ted Prim, an attorney on the Commission staff.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DBH:jh
Enclosure:(1)

COUNTY COUNSEL
EUREKA CALIFORNIA 95501     PHONE (707) 445-7235

January 12, 1976


Chairman of the
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California

Re: Request for Opinion

Dear Sir:

In accordance with the provisions of Sections 83114 of
the Government Code and 18320 of Title 2 of the California
Administrative Code, I hereby request an opinion on the matters set forth in the attached letter from Wiley J. Lacy, Jr.,
a member of the governing board of the Scotia Union School
District.

I have informed Mr. Lacy that the Conflict of Interest
Code must be submitted to and reviewed by the Board of Supervisors of the County of Humboldt, rather than the Fair
Political Practices Commission as erroneously stated in his
letter. I have also furnished Mr. Lacy with a copy of a
model Conflict of Interest Code which was developed by the
League of California Cities and the County Supervisors'
Association of California.

It is my understanding that, historically, the lumber
company has performed work on school properties and furnished
equipment and supplies to the school district without entering into written contracts with the district.

Because of the unique nature of this district, and the
fact that most, if not all, of the governing board members
are employed by the lumber company, I think it is important
that the governing board members be given direction by the
Commission as to how they are to comply with the conflict of
interest and disclosure requirements set forth in Proposition 9.

Your early consideration of this matter will be greatly
appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Raymond W. Schneider
County Counsel

cc: Wiley J. Lacy, Jr.

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]
[illegible] & [illegible]

COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT

December 31, 1975


Mr. Raymond W. Schneider
Humboldt County Counsel
825 - 5th Street
Eureka, CA 95501

Dear Mr. Schneider:

I am presently a member of the Board of Trustees of the Scotia
Union Elementary School District. It has come to my attention
that all school boards must adopt a policy in order to comply
with conflict-of-interest legislation, known as Proposition
9, which was recently passed by the people of California.

Because Scotia Union is probably the most unique school
district in the State of California, a little background is
in order.

The Pacific Lumber Company has always maintained a school in
Scotia. When it came time to replace the old school because
of the Field Act, the very finest facility with the most
modern and functional design was selected and built. This
school was built in 1970-71 school year at a cost of $500,000
to The Pacific Lumber Company. Not one cent went into this
facility from the taxpayers of the district, nor were any
funds from the County, State or Federal Government involved.

The school building and the land use for school purpose are
owned by The Pacific Lumber Company. The school board receives
no remuneration for its service. The students who attend this
school come from the town of Scotia and its neighboring
communities of Stafford, Shively, Holmes Flat, Larabee and
Redcrest.

O

The Pacific Lumber Company pays approximately 96% of the county
taxes collected within the school district. I hold a position
of management with the Company. Only a very few families live
within the district who are not either employed by the Company
or receive some remuneration from the Company. The conflict-
of-interest policy of the Scotia Union School District must be
filed with the Fair Political Practices Commission on or by
April 1, 1976 and within 30 days after the effective date of
this policy each board member shall have filed his statement
with the above commission.

As Legal Counsel for the Scotia Union Elementary School District,
I respectfully request an opinion from you on the following
questions:

1. The Pacific Lumber Company owns the school building and land,
but also owns 85% of the real estate in the district. Who
could serve on the Scotia Union board without a conflict-of-
interest?

2. What would you recommend as a minimum form, for a policy to
be adopted by the Scotia Union District, to comply with the
conflict-of-interest code?

3. Would a financial disclosure statement require the nature of
the investment or interest, as well as the name of the
business entity and a general description of the business
activity?

4. Disclosure of Income - does this require someone on the
Scotia Union board to report all income aggregating $250.00
or more and a description of the business activity and
source of said income?

Waiting your prompt reply and with best wishes for the New Year.

Yours very truly,

Wiley J. Lacy, Jr.
WJL:lw                                Box 85
                                      Scotia 95565
